items.summary: Prices are up 21% from a month ago with the oil market roiled by the U.S.-Iran war.

items.headline: Russia told Trump it has not not shared intelligence with Iran during war, Witkoff says
items.summary: U.S. officials have said Russia has given Iran information that could help its forces strike U.S. military assets in the Middle East, MS Now has reported.

items.headline: European stocks jump 2% as sliding oil prices buoy sentiment
items.summary: European equities rebounded on Tuesday as traders continued to watch developments in the Middle East.

items.headline: February home sales see small rebound, but supply growth is 'sluggish'
items.summary: Home sales made a small gain to start the year, but higher mortgage rates now could throw cold water on the spring season.

items.headline: Federal appeals court orders end to SAVE plan used by millions of student loan borrowers
items.summary: A federal appeals court ordered the end to the SAVE plan, the Biden administration-era program used by millions of student loan borrowers.

items.headline: Jim Cramer says this AI stock is "in the sweet spot" — plus, an Nvidia GTC preview
items.summary: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

items.headline: Google deepens Pentagon AI push after Anthropic sues Trump administration
items.summary: Google is rolling out a feature that lets civilian and military personnel build custom AI agents for unclassified work on the Pentagon's enterprise AI portal.

items.headline: 10-year Treasury yield is little changed as oil tumbles after Trump warns Iran over Hormuz flows
items.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield was flat as oil prices tumbled after Trump warned that Iran would be hit harder if it tried to halt Strait of Hormuz shipments.

items.headline: Nvidia makes 'significant investment' in Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab
items.summary: As part of the partnership, Thinking Machines Lab has also agreed to deploy at least one gigawatt of Nvidia's Vera Rubin systems.

items.headline: Iran war: Hegseth says Tuesday 'will be our most intense day of strikes'
items.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday had predicted that the war against Iran would be over "very soon," and warned that country against withholding oil afterward.

items.headline: IEA countries to meet later Tuesday on release of oil reserves, but no decision made yet
items.summary: The IEA's members are advanced economies in North America, Europe and Northeast Asia. They collectively hold about 1.2 billion barrels of oil in reserve.

items.headline: Amazon plans 'deep dive' internal meeting to address AI-related outages
items.summary: Amazon said AI-assisted production changes were partly to blame for recent infrastructure issues.

items.headline: Oil falls more than 10% to $84 on anticipation of emergency reserves release
items.summary: The International Energy Agency will hold an extraodrinary meeting on Tuesday to discuss a possible release of emergency stockpiles.

items.headline: South Korea opposed to U.S. moving air defense systems in the country to Middle East: President Lee
items.summary: Lee maintained that even if the assets were moved out of the country, its deterrence capability against North Korea will not be affected.

items.headline: Vertex passes key test in quest to treat kidney diseases
items.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals said its drug for a rare kidney condition succeeded in a Phase 3 trial.

items.headline: FDA approves leucovorin as first drug for rare genetic disorder, after touting it as autism treatment
items.summary: The move comes months after the Trump administration touted leucovorin as a potential therapy for a broader group of patients with autism symptoms.

items.headline: Fabletics launches denim as the athleisure trade loses steam
items.summary: Fabletics is launching a new denim assortment priced between $79.95 and $174.95 as the athleisure boom starts to slow down.

items.headline: Boaz Weinstein warns of private credit’s 'financial alchemy,' says problems are multiplying by the quarter
items.summary: Saba, alongside Cox Capital Management, launched a tender offer to purchase shares in one of Blue Owl's non-traded private credit funds at a 34.9% discount.

items.headline: Iran defends strikes on Gulf neighbors — but they say trust is broken
items.summary: Iran has defended its strikes against its Gulf countries but its neighbors say attacks have damaged trust for a long time.

items.headline: India's restaurants are under threat from the LPG supply crunch caused by the Iran war
items.summary: Restaurants in India are facing closure as the government restricts the supply of LPG as cooking fuel, industry representatives tell CNBC.

items.headline: China exports sharply beat expectations as trade surplus in the first two months surges to highest on record
items.summary: China typically combines January and February trade data to smooth distortions from the shifting Lunar New Year holiday.

items.headline: Swedish legaltech Legora hits $5 billion valuation as investors pile money into European AI startups
items.summary: The announcement comes on the back of a bumper start to the week for European AI companies.

items.headline: Warsh to meet Tillis as Senate confirmation remains blocked
items.summary: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., has refused to advance Warsh's confirmation until a probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell is dropped.

items.headline: Kevin Mandia sold his cybersecurity company to Google in 2022. He has a fresh $190 million for a new venture
items.summary: Mandiant founder Kevin Mandia is staying in cybersecurity after selling his prior company to Google, but now he's focused on AI.

items.headline: Highly successful people fail, too—but they use these 5 phrases to stay motivated, says expert
items.summary: "Beliefs peppered with self-criticism can tank motivation, but turning negative self-talk into more positive language can fuel tenacity," says Nir Eyal, author of "Beyond Belief."

items.headline: Spirit Airlines to recall furloughed pilots to combat attrition as carrier eyes bankruptcy exit
items.summary: Spirit Airlines is reversing course on pilot furloughs after aviators left in high numbers for rival airlines.

items.headline: How the Iran war and rising energy prices are threatening semiconductor demand
items.summary: A prolonged U.S.-Israel war against Iran could lead to shortages of key chipmaking materials and higher energy costs which could hurt semiconductor demand.

items.headline: Oracle earnings will show whether its expensive AI bet is starting to pay off
items.summary: Oracle's $300 billion deal with OpenAI established it as a major player in AI cloud computing alongside Amazon, Google and Microsoft.

items.headline: Olaplex stock has plunged since its IPO. Here’s how the hair care brand is trying to turn itself around
items.summary: Olaplex, a prestige hair care brand, has seen its sales and stock price plummet after a lawsuit. Now, the company is trying to turn its business around.

items.headline: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber stepping back, former WordPress.com parent chief Toni Schneider named interim boss
items.summary: Bluesky was born out of Twitter by Jack Dorsey in 2019 and rose to popularity as an alternative to X after Elon Musk's acquisition.

items.headline: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square files for IPO on the NYSE
items.summary: The planned listing would give public investors a stake in Ackman's investment platform, which oversees a concentrated portfolio of large-cap companies

items.summary: The comments come less than a week after Trump suggested that his administration would turn its sights on Havana.

items.headline: Identity theft can cost you during tax season: It's 'a terrible reverse lottery,' one victim says
items.summary: Employment-related identity theft is on the rise. Victims may not find out until tax season.

items.headline: Lego keeps beating the toy industry. Its secret weapon is not what you'd expect
items.summary: Lego on Tuesday reported a 12% jump in revenue for fiscal year 2025 and reported consumer sales that far outpace the broader toy industry.

items.headline: As Iran war disrupts oil prices, consumers could be 'hammered,' economist says
items.summary: The Iran war may impact U.S. consumers far beyond the gas pump, raising inflation expectations and causing an uptick in the yield on the 10-year Treasury note.

items.headline: Volkswagen flags a tough year ahead as 2025 profit halves on tariffs, China competition
items.summary: Volkswagen reported a more than 50% drop in annual operating profit, citing the impact of U.S. tariffs, currency effects and a strategic shift at Porsche.

items.headline: Stellantis taps Toyota, Bosch suppliers for hybrid technologies for new Jeeps
items.summary: Jeep maker Stellantis is leaning on technologies from automotive suppliers for its newest hybrid vehicles, CNBC has learned.

items.headline: China’s nudge, U.S. waiver and Iran tensions test India’s economic balancing act
items.summary: India is trying to preserve its long-standing neutral foreign policy as escalating tensions around Iran threaten its oil supply and expose New Delhi to pressure from both Washington and Beijing.

items.headline: As the Iran war upends energy flows, Russia is emerging as the real winner
items.summary: Russia is shaping up to be a major beneficiary of the escalating energy crisis in the Persian Gulf, analysts told CNBC.

items.headline: Iran war threatens catastrophic consequences for the oil market, Aramco CEO says
items.summary: Saudi Aramco beats profit estimates and keeps $85 billion payouts as oil prices surge again.

items.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Markets recover as Trump hints Iran war is nearing its end
items.summary: U.S. key benchmarks closed in the green after Trump signaled a near end to the Iran war.

items.headline: Nvidia plans open-source AI agent platform ‘NemoClaw’ for enterprises: Wired
items.summary: Nvidia is planning to lean into the agentic AI craze and launch an open-source platform for AI agents called 'NemoClaw,' according to a report.

items.headline: Anthropic sues Trump administration over Pentagon blacklist

items.headline: Elon Musk's xAI wants to build a power plant in Mississippi. Regulators plan a key meeting on Election Day
items.summary: The NAACP has accused Mississippi regulators of rushing a meeting to approve xAI plans for a massive, methane-burning power plant in Southaven.

items.headline: Top permitting-reform Republican, Democratic senators meeting as talks thaw: API chief
items.summary: Speeding up the federal permitting process is a top priority for the energy and tech industries.

items.headline: Anthropic was the Pentagon's choice for AI. Now it's banned and experts are worried
items.summary: Anthropic's partnerships with Amazon and Palantir helped it make inroads into the DOD, and its blacklisting is concerning to many industry experts.

items.headline: OpenAI to buy cybersecurity startup Promptfoo to better safeguard AI agents

items.headline: Oracle is building yesterday’s data centers with tomorrow’s debt
items.summary: The OpenAI deal fallout exposes the fundamental danger of being the most leveraged player in a market where the chip cycle moves faster than the concrete dries.

items.headline: Amazon's Zoox expands robotaxi testing to Phoenix and Dallas
items.summary: Zoox will start by deploying a fleet of retrofitted Toyota Highlander SUVs, before rolling out its toaster-shaped robotaxis for testing.

items.headline: Prediction markets face questions on Iran war bets, from regime change to nuclear detonation
items.summary: Prediction markets are facing backlash over bets related to war, with Polymarket recently archiving some predictions on nuclear detonation.

items.headline: Online age-verification tools spread across U.S. for child safety, but adults are being surveilled
items.summary: New age-verification laws and tools are designed for child safety on social media and the internet, but adults are in the crosshairs, say privacy experts.

items.headline: Microsoft adds higher-priced Office tier with Copilot as it tries to juice sales with AI
items.summary: Microsoft 365 E7 comes with Copilot, as well as identity, management and security features that might make more businesses adopt AI.
